action.reasoning: Observation: recursive filesystem scan shows /data only contains train.pt and val.pt at visible depths; no raw CSV fallback is available. Analysis: both torch.load and OGB dataset creation segfault in this environment, so standard training/prediction on provided graph files is blocked. We still need to make progress toward the mandatory eval and isolate whether an alternate deserialization path exists. PyTorch 2.11 supports weights_only and mmap options; sometimes legacy pickle paths crash while mmap/weights_only or a fresh subprocess with faulthandler can reveal more. Also, predict.py currently depends on torch.load(test.pt), which would likely fail in eval too unless we find a safe load mode. Decision: add deeper diagnostics to try multiple torch.load modes on train/val in isolated subprocesses and inspect file headers; if any mode works, pivot code accordingly next iteration. If all fail, we may need to stop because the dataset is unusable in this container.
